summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture of a blistering May ’77 Alabama show – tight playing, killer Half‑Step and legendary Scarlet > Fire, plus an all‑time great Terrapin/Playin’/Wharf Rat sequence.
review: The gd77‑05‑17.sbd.weiner recording is a high‑fidelity soundboard source that lets the band’s May‑1977 fire shine through – every instrument sits in the mix and the bass is deep without being muddy.  Listeners rave about the incendiary Mississippi Half‑Step, one of the best Scarlet > Fire stretches ever, a jaw‑dropping Jack‑a‑Roe, and a majestic second‑set flow (Samson > Delilah → Estimated Prophet → Terrapin Station → Playin’ → Wharf Rat).  Jerry’s tone and solos are described as “perfect” and “on fire,” Phil’s fretless bass grooves are highlighted repeatedly, Keith’s keyboards are “in the pocket,” and the drums (Bill & Mickey) are praised for power and swing.  A handful of reviewers note minor tonal quirks (deep bass boost, a slightly fast second set) but overall the performance is rated as one of the top Spring‑’77 concerts.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 80 % of the 120+ reviews are 4–5 stars, with consistent praise for both musical performance and soundboard quality.  The show is repeatedly called a “must‑listen” and “top‑10/5” Dead concert, while recording comments describe it as “crisp,” “clear,” or “excellent.”  This yields a high composite rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Soundboard mix is clean and well‑balanced; reviewers note crisp highs, strong midrange, deep but controlled bass.  A few mention a slight speed/pitch issue on the matrix version and minor hiss, but overall the audio is described as “crisp,” “beautiful,” and “very good.”

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is a tight, high‑energy launch with strong vocal harmonies and tight rhythm; second set builds into expansive jams – Samson > Delilah sets the tone, followed by a flawless Estimated → Terrapin/Playin’/Wharf Rat suite that many cite as one of the best May ’77 second‑set sequences.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for ‘perfect’ tone, soaring solos (especially on Scarlet, Jack‑a‑Roe and Terrapin), and tight vocal harmonies with Donna.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted for “great bass licks” on Mississippi Half‑Step, solid groove throughout, and standout moments in the second set; described as “on fire” and “in sync” with Jerry.
band_member_comments.Bob: His vocals are highlighted as “sweet,” especially on Jack Straw, Brown Eyed Women, and as a harmonic partner to Donna’s voice.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith (and later Brent) are called “right on” for fills, “in the pocket” during Half‑Step and high‑energy jams; his playing is described as “finest hour.”
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann and Mickey Hart receive praise for powerful, swinging drumming – especially on Good Lovin’, the second‑set transitions, and the Wharf Rat crescendo.
